component.name: RSPlayer
component.summary: Hi-fi music player with bit-perfect playback

description.p: RSPlayer is a music player built for high-quality playback of local music libraries. It decodes FLAC, WAV, ALAC, DSD (DSF/DFF), MP3, AAC, Ogg and more, and can send audio bit-perfect to a USB DAC via direct ALSA access, or through PipeWire/PulseAudio for everyday listening.

ul.li: Fast library scanning with rich metadata, album art and lyrics
Gapless playback, ReplayGain/loudness analysis and a parametric DSP chain
Bit-perfect exclusive output to USB DACs, including DSD
Multiroom: group RSPlayer instances on your LAN for synchronized playback

description.p: The desktop app embeds the same engine that powers the headless RSPlayer server, which you can also run on a Raspberry Pi, NAS or home server and control from any browser.

Note: by default the app can read music from your Music folder and from removable drives and mounts (/media, /run/media, /mnt). If your library lives elsewhere, grant access to that folder with Flatseal or "flatpak override". Mounting network shares from inside the app and system power actions are not available in the Flatpak sandbox.

screenshot.caption: Now Playing with real-time visualizer and multiroom control
Play queue with search and drag-and-drop reordering
Settings: playback, DSP equalizer, multiroom and library

description.p: Platform-aware playback defaults: sound works on first launch without visiting Settings. Fixes off-screen confirmation dialogs and first-launch volume issues; updated welcome dialog.
Packaging-only release: the desktop app is now available as a Snap for Ubuntu. No Flatpak changes.
Multiroom synchronized playback (beta), Windows ASIO output and first Flatpak release.
